ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON NOV-02-2011 IN 503, DEFENDANT 3M COMPANY'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL DATE. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT 3M COMPANY'S MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE IS GRANTED. THE TRIAL DATE OF FEBRUARY 6, 2012 IS VACATED AND CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 17, 2012 AT 11:15AM IN DEPT. 503. ALL DISCOVERY DATES AND SUMMARY MOTION FILING AND HEARING DATES AS THEY RELATE TO 3M ONLY TRACK THE NEW TRIAL DATE. DISCOVERY IS OPENED AS TO 3M ONLY, WHICH DOES NOT AFFECT THE CURRENT DISCOVERY AS TO DECEDENT'S WORK AT AEROJET. HOWEVER, IF THE 3M DISCOVERY REVEALS NEW EVIDENCE AGAINST THE EXISTING DEFENDANTS, THOSE DEFENDANTS WILL HAVE AN OPPORTUNITY TO PURSUE SUCH DISCOVERY AS TO THE NEW EVIDENCE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (503)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-19-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Bret R. Landess, Esq., of OIUM REYEN & PRYOR (415-392-8300), appearing for defendant / moving party CNH America LLC
Anne T. Acuna, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties George Lucas, Jr., et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT CNH AMERICA LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant CNH America LLC's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. The deposition of George Lucas, Jr. precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. While Mr. Lucas' deposition did not conclude prior to his death, defendant was afforded a meaningful opportunity to ask questions of Mr. Lucas. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR # 9225 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-19-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Deborah A. Smith, Esq. of GORDON & REES LLP (415-875-3383), appearing for defendant / moving party Gardena Holdings, Inc.
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties George Lucas, Jr., et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT GARDENA HOLDINGS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Gardena Holdings, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiffs' request for a continuance under CCP 437c(h) is denied. Mr. Hersom's declaration fails to provide any reasonable basis to believe that "facts essential to justify opposition may exist but ' [could not have been] presented" at the time plaintiffs' opposition papers were due. There is no indication in Mr. Hersom's declaration or in any other papers submitted by the plaintiffs that any of the prior depositions of defendant's PMK taken by the Brayton Purcell firm have been reviewed to determine if there are or are not any facts in those depositions that would assist in opposing this motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR # 9225 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-19-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sunny S. Shapiro, Esq., of SEDGWICK DETERT, MORAN & ARNOLD LLP (415-781-7900), appearing for defendant / moving party Caterpillar Inc.
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties George Lucas, Jr., et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT CATERPILLAR INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Caterpillar Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether George Lucas, Jr. was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant's objection to Mr. Lucas' deposition on the grounds that the deposition was not completed and/or defendant did not have adequate opportunity to examine Mr. Lucas is overruled. While Mr. Lucas' deposition did not conclude prior to his death, defendant was afforded a meaningful opportunity to ask questions of Mr. Lucas. Mr. Lucas' deposition testimony, however, does not provide any admissible evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant's authentication objection to exhibit C is sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR # 9225 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-19-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Anne T. Acuna, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties George Lucas, Jr., et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT CIBA CORPORATION, FKA CIBA SPECIALTY CHEMICALS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Ciba Corporation, FKA CIBA SPECIALTY CHEMICALS CORPORATION, SII TO THE CHEMICALS BUSINESS OF CIBA-GEIGY CORPORATION AND SUED HEREIN AS CIBA SPECIALTY CHEMICALS CORPORATION's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to prove their claims. By not presenting plaintiffs' responses to specially-prepared contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant is unable to successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR # 9225 = (220/HEK)
